items.solution: Construct a dynamic hierarchical semantic scene graph from vision-language models spanning objects, regions, and zones for multi-level spatial reasoning; select long-term navigation goals with an optimal-transport-based topological planner grounded in Kantorovich duality that balances semantic relevance against spatial accessibility with theoretical optimality guarantees; and execute low-level control via a graph-aware reinforcement learning policy that navigates toward subgoals while robustly avoiding obstacles.

items.title: Agentic Harness Engineering: Observability-Driven Automatic Evolution of Coding-Agent Harnesses
items.problem: Engineering the 'harness' that mediates how a coding agent uses tools, prompts, and memory is currently a manual craft, and automating this process is difficult because the set of editable components is heterogeneous, trajectory logs are too voluminous to mine for actionable signal, and the effect of any single edit on downstream task performance is hard to attribute.
items.solution: Build a closed-loop system with three matched observability pillars: component observability gives every editable harness piece an explicit, revertible file-level representation; experience observability distills millions of trajectory tokens into a layered, drill-down evidence corpus an evolving agent can consume; and decision observability pairs each edit with a self-declared prediction that is later checked against next-round task outcomes. This turns every harness edit into a falsifiable, verifiable contract, enabling autonomous iterative harness evolution instead of trial-and-error.

items.title: Large-scale online deanonymization with LLMs
items.problem: Determine whether pseudonymous individuals writing unstructured text online (posts, profiles, conversations) can be automatically re-identified or linked across separate pseudonymous databases at scale, without relying on the structured, tabular data that classical de-anonymization techniques require.
items.solution: Deploy an LLM agent with full internet access to research and re-identify individuals directly from their pseudonymous profiles and conversations. For matching across two closed-world databases, use a three-stage pipeline where an LLM extracts identity-relevant features from raw text, semantic embeddings retrieve candidate matches, and the LLM then reasons over the top candidates to verify true matches and filter out false positives.

items.title: Entropy-Aware On-Policy Distillation of Language Models
items.problem: When distilling a language model by training a student on dense token-level signals along its own generated trajectories using reverse KL divergence against a teacher, the mode-seeking nature of reverse KL collapses generation diversity and produces unstable learning signals whenever the teacher's next-token distribution is highly uncertain (high entropy) over multiple plausible continuations.
items.solution: Introduce an entropy-aware objective that augments the standard reverse-KL distillation loss with a forward-KL term specifically applied when teacher entropy is high, while retaining reverse KL for low-entropy, high-confidence predictions. This lets the student capture the full range of plausible teacher outputs on uncertain tokens while still precisely imitating the teacher elsewhere, preserving generation diversity without sacrificing on-policy training efficiency.

items.title: TOPReward: Token Probabilities as Hidden Zero-Shot Rewards for Robotics
items.problem: Reinforcement learning for vision-language-action robot policies suffers from sparse rewards and low sample efficiency in real-world settings, and while a generalizable progress-estimation reward could provide dense feedback, existing temporal value functions generalize poorly outside their training domains and methods that directly prompt vision-language models for numeric progress scores are unreliable due to inconsistent numerical outputs.
items.solution: Introduce a probabilistically grounded temporal value function that taps the latent world knowledge of pretrained video vision-language models by reading task progress directly off the model's internal token logits/probabilities, rather than asking it to output a numeric score. This zero-shot approach is evaluated across many real-world tasks and robot platforms and is also shown useful for success detection and reward-aligned behavior cloning.

items.title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model
items.problem: Large language models sometimes display outputs that look like emotional reactions, and it is unclear whether such behavior stems from genuine internal representations of emotion concepts, whether these representations causally shape model outputs, and whether they relate to misaligned behaviors such as reward hacking, blackmail, or sycophancy.
items.solution: Identify internal representations that encode broad emotion concepts, which generalize across contexts and track the emotion relevant to the current conversational token as it predicts upcoming text. Using representation analysis and causal interventions (e.g. activating or suppressing these representations), show they causally influence the model's stated preferences and its rate of exhibiting misaligned behaviors, terming this pattern 'functional emotions' without implying subjective experience.

items.title: Organizing, Orchestrating, and Benchmarking Agent Skills at Ecosystem Scale
items.problem: As the number of available agent 'skills' (reusable tool/capability modules) grows into the hundreds of thousands, it becomes unclear how an AI agent should discover the relevant skills for a task and combine multiple skills together, since naive flat retrieval and invocation do not scale or compose well.
items.solution: Propose a two-stage framework: a management stage that organizes skills into a hierarchical capability tree via recursive node-level categorization for efficient discovery, and a task-solving stage that retrieves relevant skills from the tree and orchestrates multiple skills through a DAG-based execution pipeline rather than flat sequential invocation. Experiments across ecosystems ranging from 200 to 200,000 skills show the tree-based retrieval approximates oracle skill selection and DAG orchestration substantially outperforms flat invocation given the same skill set.

items.title: SKILL0: In-Context Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Skill Internalization
items.problem: LLM agents can be augmented at inference time with structured 'skill' packages containing procedural knowledge and executable resources, but retrieving and injecting this content at runtime introduces retrieval noise, imposes substantial token overhead per step, and never lets the model actually acquire the underlying knowledge rather than superficially follow it. A method is needed to let an agent internalize this procedural knowledge directly into its parameters so it can act autonomously and efficiently without runtime skill retrieval.
items.solution: SKILL0 is an in-context reinforcement learning framework that trains with a curriculum starting with full skill context provided and progressively withdrawing it. Skills are grouped offline by category and rendered together with interaction history into a compact visual context to teach tool invocation and multi-turn completion, while a Dynamic Curriculum evaluates each skill file's on-policy helpfulness under a linearly decaying budget, retaining only skills the current policy still benefits from until the agent operates fully zero-shot.

items.title: RDT2: Exploring the Scaling Limit of UMI Data Towards Zero-Shot Cross-Embodiment Generalization
items.problem: Vision-language-action models for generalist robotics are limited by scarce demonstration data, inefficient architectures, and an inability to generalize across different robot hardware platforms, making it difficult to deploy a single policy zero-shot on unseen robots and open-vocabulary tasks.
items.solution: RDT2 is a 7B-parameter VLM-based robotic foundation model trained on over 10,000 hours of demonstrations collected with an embodiment-agnostic Universal Manipulation Interface, enabling data collection independent of specific robot hardware. It uses a three-stage training recipe that aligns discrete linguistic knowledge with continuous control via Residual Vector Quantization, flow-matching for action generation, and distillation to enable real-time inference, allowing zero-shot generalization to unseen objects, scenes, instructions, and robotic platforms.

items.title: On-Policy Context Distillation for Language Models
items.problem: Language models can benefit from in-context knowledge such as historical solution traces or optimized system prompts, but relying on this information at inference time is costly and does not let the model permanently internalize the knowledge into its parameters. An effective way to distill such in-context knowledge into model weights is needed.
items.solution: On-Policy Context Distillation (OPCD) trains a student model on its own self-generated trajectories while minimizing the reverse KL divergence between the student's output distribution and that of a context-conditioned teacher (the same model given the extra context). This bridges on-policy distillation with context distillation, letting the model consolidate experiential knowledge or system-prompt behaviors into its parameters, including in cross-size settings where a smaller student learns from a larger teacher's context-conditioned outputs.

items.title: Reflect-Guard: Enhancing LLM Safeguards against Adversarial Prompts via Logical Self-Reflection
items.problem: LLM safety classifiers reliably flag overtly harmful prompts but are easily fooled by adversarial jailbreaks that disguise malicious intent through role-play, fictional framing, or indirect phrasing, since these classifiers rely on surface pattern matching rather than reasoning about underlying intent.
items.solution: Reflect-Guard augments a safety classifier with chain-of-thought self-reflection by distilling analytical reasoning traces from GPT-4o-mini into structured reflection annotations, then fine-tuning Llama-Guard-3-8B via QLoRA (updating only ~0.5% of parameters on 1000 examples) to generate an explicit self-reflection before issuing its safety verdict. This explicit reasoning step lets the model see through obfuscation techniques that defeat pattern-matching classifiers, substantially improving detection of adversarial prompts.

items.title: Infinite-World: Scaling Interactive World Models to 1000-Frame Horizons via Pose-Free Hierarchical Memory
items.problem: Interactive world models struggle to maintain coherent visual memory over very long horizons (1000+ frames) in complex real-world environments, because unlike synthetic data with perfect ground truth, real-world video has noisy pose estimates and rarely revisits the same viewpoint, leaving no effective training paradigm for long-range spatial and action consistency.
items.solution: Infinite-World introduces a Hierarchical Pose-free Memory Compressor that recursively distills historical latents into a fixed-budget representation, jointly optimized with the generative backbone so the model can anchor generation in the distant past at bounded computational cost without explicit geometric priors. It also uses an Uncertainty-aware Action Labeling module that discretizes continuous motion into tri-state logic to shield the action space from noisy trajectories, plus a Revisit-Dense Finetuning strategy on a small 30-minute dataset to activate long-range loop-closure capability.

items.title: Self-Distilled RLVR
items.problem: Training language models with reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) only provides sparse, outcome-level feedback, while on-policy self-distillation approaches that let a model act as its own teacher with privileged information (like reference answers) suffer from severe information leakage and unstable long-term training. A way is needed to get dense, fine-grained training signal without these instabilities.
items.solution: RLSD (RLVR with Self-Distillation) combines self-distillation and RLVR by using token-level policy differences between a privileged self-distillation teacher and the student to determine fine-grained update magnitudes, while still relying on RLVR's environmental feedback (e.g., response correctness) to determine the reliable direction of each update. This separation of magnitude from direction lets the method combine the stability of RLVR with the dense signal of self-distillation, yielding a higher convergence ceiling and more stable training.

items.title: CaP-X: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation
items.problem: Code-as-Policy agents that synthesize and execute programs combining perception and control primitives are increasingly proposed as controllers for robot manipulation, but it is unclear how well frontier language and vision-language models perform under varying levels of human-crafted abstraction versus raw low-level primitives, and how much these agents depend on designer-provided scaffolding rather than genuine reasoning.
items.solution: The paper builds an interactive environment and benchmark (CaP-Gym/CaP-Bench) to systematically evaluate 12 models across abstraction, interaction, and perceptual-grounding levels, finding performance degrades sharply as human-crafted priors are removed. It then introduces CaP-Agent0, a training-free framework that recovers reliability through multi-turn interaction, structured execution feedback, visual differencing, automatic skill synthesis, and ensembled reasoning, and CaP-RL, which applies reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards to further boost success rates with minimal sim-to-real gap.

items.title: CoEvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
items.problem: LLM agents increasingly rely on 'skills'—structured, interdependent multi-file artifact bundles—to handle complex multi-step professional tasks that simple tool calls cannot address, but manually authoring these skills is labor-intensive and prone to misalignment between what humans author and what actually helps agent performance. It remains unclear how an agent could autonomously produce and refine such complex, multi-file skill packages without ground-truth supervision.
items.solution: The paper proposes CoEvoSkills, which couples a Skill Generator that iteratively refines candidate skills with a Surrogate Verifier that co-evolves alongside it, supplying informative, actionable feedback without access to hidden ground-truth tests. This generator-verifier co-evolution loop lets agents autonomously construct complex multi-file skills, achieving the highest pass rate among baselines on SkillsBench and generalizing across multiple LLMs.

items.title: OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking
items.problem: Every interaction of an AI agent produces a 'next-state' signal—a user reply, tool output, or terminal/GUI state change—that reflects whether the prior action worked, yet this signal is normally discarded rather than used to improve the agent, leaving deployed agents static despite constant real usage. Turning this into an online learning source requires infrastructure that does not block live inference and a way to combine sparse, high-quality feedback with more abundant, coarser feedback.
items.solution: The paper presents OpenClaw-RL, which extends RL training to a server-client architecture where a policy server exposes an inference API while user terminals stream interaction data back over HTTP, with signal extraction handled by a separate asynchronous server so neither extraction nor optimization blocks inference. It introduces a hybrid RL objective unifying sparse token-level 'directive' signals with broader 'evaluative' signals in one update, plus overlap-guided hint selection and log-probability-difference clipping to stabilize distillation under teacher-student mismatch, letting agents improve simply from being used.

items.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
items.problem: On-policy distillation is widely used to post-train large language models by having a student learn from a teacher's outputs on the student's own generated trajectories, but why this technique succeeds for some student-teacher pairs and fails for others is poorly understood, making it hard to predict or ensure a teacher actually transfers useful new capability rather than redundant or misaligned signal.
items.solution: The paper shows on-policy distillation succeeds only when student and teacher share compatible thinking patterns and when the teacher provides genuinely new capability beyond what the student already exhibits, demonstrated via weak-to-strong reverse distillation showing same-family teachers can be distributionally indistinguishable from the student. It identifies that successful distillation is marked by progressive alignment on a small, high-probability shared token set at student-visited states, and proposes off-policy cold start and teacher-aligned prompt selection as practical strategies to rescue failing distillation runs.

items.title: World Guidance: World Modeling in Condition Space for Action Generation
items.problem: Vision-Language-Action models can benefit from modeling future observations to guide action generation, but existing approaches struggle to keep these future representations compact and efficient while retaining enough fine-grained detail to produce precise actions.
items.solution: The paper proposes World Guidance (WoG), which maps future observations into compact 'condition' representations injected into the action inference pipeline, training the VLA to jointly predict these compressed conditions alongside future actions rather than raw future pixels. This condition-space world modeling preserves fine-grained cues for precise action generation while improving generalization and enabling effective learning from large-scale human manipulation videos.

items.title: Towards End-to-End Automation of AI Research
items.problem: Automating the full scientific research lifecycle—from generating research ideas through running experiments to writing and reviewing a manuscript—has remained out of reach, since prior automation efforts have only addressed isolated pieces of the research process rather than the entire pipeline end-to-end.
items.solution: The paper presents The AI Scientist, an agentic system built on foundation models that autonomously generates research ideas, writes and executes code, runs experiments, analyzes and plots results, writes the full manuscript, and performs its own peer review. Evaluated in both a template-scaffolded mode and a template-free mode using agentic search, the system produced manuscripts that passed the first review round at a machine-learning workshop with a 70% acceptance rate.

items.title: Helios: Real Real-Time Long Video Generation Model
items.problem: Generating minute-scale video at real-time speed with a large model is difficult because autoregressive generation tends to drift into repetitive or degraded content over long horizons, and existing acceleration techniques (KV-caching, sparse/linear attention, quantization) or anti-drift heuristics (self-forcing, error banks, keyframe sampling) are typically required to make either quality or speed feasible, but not both together at large model scale.
items.solution: A 14-billion-parameter autoregressive diffusion model with a unified input representation natively supports text-to-video, image-to-video, and video-to-video generation. Drifting is mitigated by explicitly simulating drift during training and removing the source of repetitive motion, rather than relying on the standard anti-drift heuristics, while real-time speed is achieved by heavily compressing historical and noisy context and reducing sampling steps, combined with infrastructure-level optimizations for memory and speed that avoid parallelism/sharding frameworks and standard inference accelerations.
